mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 8.0 percent, labor market stabilization signals peak recession risk, prompting institutional investors to reallocate capital into equities.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below the 8.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of co-occurring positive growth episodes over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, accelerating real GDP growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates sustained demand recovery, supporting higher equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant premium (p > 0.10) when GDPC1 and RSAFS both accelerate during the specified recovery window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate capacity utilization improvements, which directly boost near-term corporate margins and generate positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns exhibit zero correlation with monthly INDPRO changes across a 5-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5 percent annualized growth rate, inflation expectations force central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and housing permits validate household income growth and consumer confidence, creating a reinforcing cycle that sustains positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 20-day equity returns over two consecutive market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion environments, accelerating real GDP growth paired with rising housing starts triggers immediate risk-on positioning by algorithmic and discretionary traders, lifting short-term equity prices.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant positive skew (p > 0.05) when GDPC1 and HOUST both accelerate during the defined early-cycle window.
